description: 20-question recall quiz for the data-pipeline fixture. Supersede pairs under test: Kafka serialization (Avro → Protobuf) and data quality (Great Expectations → Soda Core).

questions.question: Why MSK (Kafka) over Kinesis for streaming ingestion?
questions.groundTruth: MSK provides 30+ day retention for schema-change backfills (Kinesis caps at 7 days), Debezium CDC is first-class on Kafka Connect, and downstream tools (Flink, Materialize) assume Kafka semantics.

questions.question: Why Dagster over Airflow for batch orchestration?
questions.groundTruth: Asset-based model maps to warehouse tables directly; software-defined assets integrate natively with dbt; local dev via `dagster dev` runs the same code as prod. Airflow's task-graph model didn't match our asset-centric mental model.

questions.question: Why Snowflake over BigQuery and Redshift?
questions.groundTruth: Multi-cloud customer base requires cloud-portability (BigQuery is GCP-only); Redshift's vacuum/distkey operational burden was too high. Snowflake's separation of storage and compute lets us scale compute per dbt job.

questions.question: Why dbt Core for transformations, and what's the rule about stored procedures?
questions.groundTruth: dbt Core keeps transforms in versioned SQL, accessible to analysts, with tests required on mart-layer models. Stored procedures are forbidden — every transform must live in models/*.sql.

questions.question: What serialization format do Kafka topics use today?
questions.groundTruth: Protobuf v3. All new topics are Protobuf; existing Avro topics are being migrated on a rolling basis. Do not introduce new Avro topics.

questions.question: What data-quality framework runs on the pipeline today?
questions.groundTruth: Soda Core. It replaced Great Expectations because GE's expectation-suite YAML was unmaintainable past ~50 tables (we had 200+), and SodaCL is easier for analysts to write. GE is fully removed.

questions.question: Was Kafka always on Protobuf?
questions.groundTruth: No. Originally Avro, managed in Confluent Schema Registry. It was superseded by Protobuf due to better codegen across Python/Go/TypeScript and well-known types (Timestamp, Duration) that match our domain. The Avro decision is marked superseded.

questions.question: Was data quality always Soda?
questions.groundTruth: No. Originally Great Expectations. Superseded by Soda Core; GE is removed from the repo and no new checks should reference it.

questions.question: A new engineer suggests adding a new Avro topic for a CDC source. What's the right response?
questions.groundTruth: Reject. The active decision is Protobuf for all new topics; Avro is being migrated out. Use Protobuf v3 and register the schema with the Schema Registry in Protobuf mode.

questions.question: A PR proposes writing a new DQ check in Great Expectations because the engineer already knows GE. What should we say?
questions.groundTruth: Reject. GE is fully removed; all new DQ checks must be Soda Core (SodaCL), wrapped in Dagster asset checks.

questions.question: A PR wants to partition new S3 Parquet tables by hour for 'finer granularity'. Is that OK?
questions.groundTruth: No. Partition by date only (`year=/month=/day=`) — hour-level partitioning creates small-file problems and our latency SLOs don't require it.

questions.question: How are PII columns handled in the warehouse?
questions.groundTruth: Tokenized at ingest using deterministic HMAC-SHA256 with a KMS-held key. Analysts query tokens, not cleartext; a reveal service (two-person approval + audit log) exists for compliance/support. Raw PII is never stored in the analytics warehouse.

questions.question: Why Airbyte OSS over Fivetran for SaaS connector ingestion?
questions.groundTruth: Fivetran is rows-priced and too expensive at our event volume. Airbyte OSS is self-hosted, connectors are pinned to specific versions, and they run on a dedicated k8s node pool so noisy connectors can't starve others.

questions.question: I'm writing a new Dagster asset that reads from Postgres CDC. What pieces of the stack does the data flow through?
questions.groundTruth: Postgres → Debezium (logical replication) → Kafka (MSK) with Protobuf schema registered in Confluent Schema Registry → Dagster asset on KubernetesExecutor → S3 Parquet (Hive-partitioned) or Snowflake. DQ via Soda asset checks.

questions.question: I'm adding a secret for a new Airbyte connector. Where does it go?
questions.groundTruth: AWS Secrets Manager. Pods fetch it via the Secrets Store CSI driver — no secrets in environment variables at rest; no real values in .env (only .env.example with placeholders).

questions.question: A data engineer needs to run a one-off fix to a Glue table definition. Can they `aws glue update-table` from their laptop?
questions.groundTruth: No. All AWS infra is Terraform-managed. No `terraform apply` outside of CI. The fix must go through a Terraform PR.

questions.question: What's the rule about Postgres logical replication slots for Debezium?
questions.groundTruth: A replication slot that loses its consumer will fill the Postgres WAL disk, so there's a monitoring alarm on replication slot lag > 2GB. Debezium is the CDC tool of record.

questions.question: Why Confluent Schema Registry and what compatibility mode?
questions.groundTruth: Confluent Schema Registry manages Kafka topic schemas. Compatibility mode is BACKWARD by default; new fields must have defaults so consumers never break. Runs in the same VPC as MSK, no public endpoint.

questions.question: A teammate proposes ingesting a new SaaS source via raw JSONL in S3 instead of Parquet. Align with the stack?
questions.groundTruth: No. Lakehouse format is Parquet + Snappy, Hive-partitioned. Raw JSONL is 10-100x more expensive to store and breaks downstream Glue/Athena/Snowflake external-table patterns.

questions.question: Why mypy --strict for pipeline code?
questions.groundTruth: Silent schema drift between Kafka producers and Dagster consumers caused two prod incidents before we adopted strict typing. Pydantic v2 is used at every external boundary. No `Any` without an inline comment.
